title: JRN Probe Cost-Efficiency & Ranking Stability on rel-f1
summary: Demo notebook analyzing the Join Reproduction Number (JRN) probe methodology for cost-efficient join subset selection in relational databases. Uses the rel-f1 (Formula 1) dataset with 13 FK joins and 3 driver prediction tasks. Demonstrates: (A) convergence analysis showing JRN ranking stability (Spearman ρ > 0.9) across 64 budget configurations, (B) wall-clock cost comparison of JRN probe vs greedy forward selection, exhaustive search, and random search (JRN is 10-20x faster), and breakeven analysis of theoretical scaling (O(J+1) vs O(J²/2) vs O(2^J)).
